label: Saving
title: Saving
lead: Extend the vanilla save.sv flow with versioned CUCoreLib save providers.
bodyHtml:
How CUCoreLib saving works

CUCoreLib hooks the normal vanilla SaveSystem.SaveGame() and SaveSystem.TryLoadGame() flow, then stores a CUCoreLib object inside the existing compressed save.sv JSON.

On save, vanilla writes the run first, then CUCoreLib reopens save.sv and appends its payload. On load, CUCoreLib reads that payload before vanilla finishes loading, then restores it after the player body and world exist.

What is automatic

CUCoreLib already patches vanilla load so most of it's own registries can resolve back into generated runtime prefabs. I.e., registered custom items, liquids, body/limb statuses, settings, etc.. can survive the normal item save/load flow without you serializing the whole item yourself.

CUCoreLib also registers a small set of built-in providers for its own systems. Right now that mainly means custom building entities and a small amount of extra custom item runtime state such as light on/off state.

However, do not assume your mod's own data is saved automatically just because the item or building itself reloads. Extra instance data, global mod data, custom player state, and custom world systems still need your own save provider, explained further down.

What is JObject?

A JObject is a JSON object that (in our case) represents the serialized state of a mod's custom data. It is used to store and retrieve complex data structures that cannot be directly serialized by the vanilla save system.

For more info, see https://kodershop.com/blog/dot-net-tutorials-15/what-is-jobject-in-json-net-345#blog_content

Register a provider

- Sorry, this section is a bit more complicated for now. I'll see about abstracting this into a simpler API.

To use this for now, see the right pane for an example with JObject.


public static class DescriptionSaveManager
{
    // todo, check the right pane for an example of a saved teleport command provider
}
